# Synthetic eval-data generation

**Problem:** Hand-curating golden Q&A doesn't scale. A 30-question set takes a day; a 1000-question set is a month. Synthetic Q&A — generated by an LLM from your own corpus — fills the gap. Done well, the synthetic set tracks the golden set closely enough to drive automated regression testing.

**What you'll learn:**
- A simple **doc → Q&A** generator: one LLM call per document, JSON-structured output.
- Three **filters** that catch garbage: empty answer, answer-not-in-source, question-length sanity.
- A small **calibration step**: compare synthetic-set retrieval recall to golden-set retrieval recall; if the gap is > 0.2 the synthetic set is too easy / too hard.
- Why **diversity** matters: a single template produces near-duplicate questions, and your eval ends up measuring the template, not the retriever.

**When to use it:** Bootstrapping a new corpus before hand-curation is feasible. Continuous regression testing where you want a constantly-refreshing eval set.

**When NOT to use it:** When you have real production traffic with labels — that's strictly better than synthetic. Adversarial / red-team evaluation — humans still beat LLMs at finding edge cases.

## Run it

```powershell
uv sync --group evals
uv run jupyter lab 05-evals-and-observability/04-synthetic-eval-data/notebook.ipynb
uv run python 05-evals-and-observability/04-synthetic-eval-data/eval.py
```

Uses cached LLM calls (`LLM_CACHE_ONLY=1`) by default; the deterministic fallback emits a templated Q&A so the snapshot is reproducible.

## Key results

See [`eval-snapshot.json`](https://github.com/your-handle/agentic-ai-engineering/blob/main/05-evals-and-observability/04-synthetic-eval-data/./eval-snapshot.json) for: `n_generated`, `n_filtered_out` per filter, and the **calibration gap** between synthetic-set retrieval recall@3 and golden-set recall@3 on the canonical corpus.

## References

- [RAGAS TestSet Generator](https://docs.ragas.io/en/stable/concepts/testset_generation.html)
- Liu et al., [The Quack Test: Use synthetic eval data, but verify](https://arxiv.org/abs/2305.13691)
- LangChain, [Synthetic dataset generation for evals](https://blog.langchain.dev/synthetic-data-rag/)
